nodes.title: Split stream into clean NDJSON lines
nodes.why: A transform stage lets us buffer partial chunks and emit complete lines only.
nodes.alternative: Manually split lines inside the main read loop.
nodes.tradeoff: Very modular pipeline design, with a little extra setup code.

nodes.title: Decode bytes and split lines in a pipeline
nodes.why: This converts bytes to text before line processing, which is exactly what NDJSON needs.
nodes.tradeoff: Cleaner flow with built-ins, but depends on Web Streams APIs being available.

nodes.title: Isolate bad lines without stopping parse
nodes.why: Malformed lines are reported through onError, while valid lines keep processing.
nodes.alternative: Throw immediately on first parse failure.
nodes.tradeoff: More resilient for mixed-quality data, with optional error handling logic.

nodes.title: Use reader + TextDecoder manually

anchor.snippet: const reader = stream.getReader(); const dec = new TextDecoder();

nodes.why: This gives direct control over chunk reads and decode timing.
nodes.alternative: Use TextDecoderStream in a pipeThrough chain.
nodes.tradeoff: Flexible and lightweight, but more low-level logic in one function.

nodes.title: Extract line parsing into drain helper
nodes.why: A reusable drain function keeps the read loop short and avoids repeated parsing code.
nodes.alternative: Inline split/parse logic every time data is appended.
nodes.tradeoff: Better readability, with one extra local function.

nodes.title: Flush decoder and parse final tail
nodes.why: Final decode flush ensures trailing UTF-8 bytes and last line are not lost.
nodes.alternative: Stop after done without a final decode call.
nodes.tradeoff: More correct at stream end, with a small extra finish step.

nodes.title: Validate stream shape early
nodes.why: Failing fast with a clear message makes misuse easier to debug.
nodes.alternative: Assume valid input and let runtime fail later.
nodes.tradeoff: Better diagnostics, with one upfront guard.

nodes.title: Drain complete lines from a rolling buffer
nodes.why: Chunk boundaries may split lines, so buffering + draining keeps parsing correct.
nodes.tradeoff: Correct NDJSON behavior, with more state management.

nodes.title: Parse final tail safely after loop ends
nodes.why: The last line may arrive without trailing newline, so we parse it explicitly at the end.
nodes.alternative: Require every stream to end with newline.
nodes.tradeoff: More robust in real feeds, with a small finalization block.
